tests.source_program:
define i8 @wider_comparison_narrower_result(i64 %x, i64 %y) {
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %x, %y
  %ne = icmp ne i64 %x, %y
  %ext = zext i1 %ne to i8
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i8 -1, i8 %ext
  ret i8 %sel
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions integer comparisons but doesn't specify behavior when comparison operands (i64) differ in bit width from result type (i8). The implementation handles this via SI.getType() for the intrinsic result.

alive2.src:
define i8 @wider_comparison_narrower_result(i64 %x, i64 %y) {
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %x, %y
  %ne = icmp ne i64 %x, %y
  %ext = zext i1 %ne to i8
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i8 -1, i8 %ext
  ret i8 %sel
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i8 @wider_comparison_narrower_result(i64 %x, i64 %y) {
#0:
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %x, %y
  %ne = icmp ne i64 %x, %y
  %ext = zext i1 %ne to i8
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i8 255, i8 %ext
  ret i8 %sel
}
=>
define i8 @wider_comparison_narrower_result(i64 %x, i64 %y) {
#0:
  %sel = scmp i8 i64 %x, %y
  %#range_0_%sel = !range i8 %sel, i8 255, i8 2
  ret i8 %#range_0_%sel
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

tests.source_program:
define i8 @constant_operand_comparison(i32 %x) {
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %x, 42
  %ne = icmp ne i32 %x, 42
  %ext = zext i1 %ne to i8
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i8 -1, i8 %ext
  ret i8 %sel
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary describes pattern with variables x and y but doesn't mention cases where one comparison operand is a constant integer. The m_Value matcher in the implementation accepts constants.

alive2.src:
define i8 @constant_operand_comparison(i32 %x) {
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %x, 42
  %ne = icmp ne i32 %x, 42
  %ext = zext i1 %ne to i8
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i8 -1, i8 %ext
  ret i8 %sel
}

tests.source_program:
define <4 x i8> @wider_vector_width(<4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y) {
  %cmp = icmp slt <4 x i32> %x, %y
  %ne = icmp ne <4 x i32> %x, %y
  %ext = zext <4 x i1> %ne to <4 x i8>
  %sel = select <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i8> <i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1>, <4 x i8> %ext
  ret <4 x i8> %sel
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions vector support but existing tests only cover <2 x> vectors. This tests <4 x> vector width which is outside the documented examples but supported by the implementation.

alive2.src:
define <4 x i8> @wider_vector_width(<4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y) {
  %cmp = icmp slt <4 x i32> %x, %y
  %ne = icmp ne <4 x i32> %x, %y
  %ext = zext <4 x i1> %ne to <4 x i8>
  %sel = select <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i8> <i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1>, <4 x i8> %ext
  ret <4 x i8> %sel
}

define <4 x i8> @wider_vector_width(<4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y) {

  %ne = icmp ne <4 x i32> %x, %y
  %ext = zext <4 x i1> %ne to <4 x i8>
  %sel = select <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i8> { 255, 255, 255, 255 }, <4 x i8> %ext
  ret <4 x i8> %sel
}
=>
define <4 x i8> @wider_vector_width(<4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y) {

  %sel = scmp <4 x i8> <4 x i32> %x, %y
  %#range_0_%sel = !range <4 x i8> %sel, i8 255, i8 2
  ret <4 x i8> %#range_0_%sel
}
